channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The DJI Osmo Action 6 can now record 8K video
item.description: A new update is rolling out now for the DJI Osmo Action 6 that brings with it a host of improvements, including 8K.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI app for finding local food
item.description: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI-powered social app that recommends restaurants through conversational search, now testing in San Francisco and New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch The Hunting Wives online for FREE from anywhere
item.description: Want to watch The Hunting Wives for FREE from anywhere? Stream every new twist as Sophie is drawn into betrayal, hidden agendas, and a fight to prove her innocence.
item.dc:creator: Krishi Chowdhary, TechRadar

item.title: From OpenAI to Nvidia, here’s a list of recent multibillion-dollar AI deals
item.description: Nvidia has agreed to license technology from AI startup Groq for use in some of its artificial intelligence chips, marking the chipmaker’s largest deal and underscoring its push to strengthen competitiveness amid surging demand.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025
item.description: Federal IT and acquisition experts say the FedRAMP update and artificial intelligence pilots moving to production were among the big story lines last year. The post Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: How we can live with AI without losing our humanity
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter covers the latest artificial intelligence technology advancements, including the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuatro recomendaciones para que los CIO salven la brecha de confianza en la IA
item.description: Por tercer año consecutivo, el Thinkers360 AI Trust Index ha tomado el pulso a la opinión pública sobre la IA y, una vez más, sus resultados recuerdan a los CIO y CXO que la curva de innovación tecnológica sigue avanzando más rápido que la estructura ética y de gobernanza necesaria para sostenerla.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is S/4HANA? SAP’s latest ERP system explained
item.description: What is S/4HANA? S/4HANA is SAP’s latest iteration of its flagship enterprise resource planning (ERP) system. The successor to SAP ECC, S/4HANA is built on an in-memory database and is designed to enable real-time data processing and analysis for businesses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: After LLMs and agents, the next AI frontier: video language models
item.description: Tesla’s viral videos show its Optimus humanoid robot serving drinks to guests — a glimpse of AI in the real world that a new AI innovation, called world models, is expected to make more reliable. (For one, humanoid robots will do a better job in navigating and serving people their custom drinks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch Australia vs England: live stream Boxing Day 4th Ashes Test for *FREE* from anywhere in the world, Australia lead by 46 runs after 20 wickets fall on Day 1
item.description: All the ways to watch the 4th Ashes Test at the MCG, the famous Boxing Day Test, from anywhere as Australia and England battle in cricket’s historic series.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone scam tricks owners into giving phones away
item.description: New iPhone replacement scam uses pressure tactics and fake carrier calls to steal devices from buyers. Criminals claim shipping errors and demand urgent returns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 mandates for CIOs to bridge the AI trust gap
item.description: For the third consecutive year, the Thinkers360 AI Trust Index has taken the pulse of sentiment toward AI, and the results, once again, are a stark reminder to CIOs and CXOs that the technological innovation curve continues to outpace the ethical and governance structure required to support it.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Is the Internet's time wrong? NIST warns servers may have lapsed due to a power outage
item.description: NIST warns specific public time servers may be inaccurate after a power outage disrupted atomic clock distribution.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: New malware can read your chats and steal your money
item.description: New Android banking trojan Sturnus steals credentials, reads encrypted messages and controls devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft is not rewriting Windows in Rust
item.description: A job posting by a Microsoft engineer sparked excitement about a project “to eliminate every line of C and C++ from Microsoft by 2030”, replacing it with Rust — but alas for fans of the memory-safe programming language, it turns out this is a personal goal, not a corporate one, and Rust isn’t necess
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Pro-Russian hackers claim cyberattack on French postal service
item.description: French prosecutors say a pro-Russian hacking group has claimed responsibility for a major cyberattack on France’s national postal service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Waymo went down in San Francisco
item.description: Last weekend, a gnarly power outage in San Francisco took out a number of traffic lights, which, in turn, sent a number of self-driving Waymo robotaxis into a sort of fugue state.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: FCC bans new foreign-made drones over national security concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission on Monday said it would ban new foreign-made drones, a move that will keep new Chinese-made drones such as those from DJI and Autel out of the U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Rust vision group seeks enumeration of language design goals
item.description: To help the Rust language continue scaling across domains and usage levels, the Rust Vision Doc group recommends enumerating the design goals for evolving the language while also improving the crates package system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Android Sound Notifications help you catch key alerts
item.description: Discover Android's new Sound Notifications feature that alerts you to smoke alarms, doorbells, and baby cries even when wearing headphones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a former Forest Service employee changed the future of housing in California
item.description: One April night eight years ago, two tech leaders sat down with a former Forest Service employee at Terroir, a natural wine bar in San Francisco. Then they started sketching out a plan that would eventually reshape California’s housing policy.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: WhatsApp API worked exactly as promised, and stole everything
item.description: Security researchers have uncovered a malicious npm package that poses as a legitimate WhatsApp Web API library while quietly stealing messages, credentials, and contact data from developer environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US cyber progress isn’t stalled
item.description: Beneath the policy layer, the technical and strategic modernization of U.S. cybersecurity is actually accelerating faster than ever. The post US cyber progress isn’t stalled — it’s evolving first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Sean Davis, Federal News Network

item.title: Firefox gets a kill switch for AI features
item.description: Recently, Mozilla’s new CEO Anthony Enzor-DeMeo promised that Firefox will be a “modern AI browser,” which attracted a lot of attention. Fortunately, users who are not interested in the AI features will be able to turn it all off. – There will be an option in Firefox to disable all AI features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cursor owner Anysphere agrees to buy Graphite code review tool
item.description: Anysphere, the developer of AI coding assistant Cursor, is adding some code review and debugging skills to its portfolio with the acquisition of Graphite, TechCrunch reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Building AI agents the safe way
item.description: If you want to know what is actually happening in generative AI—not what vendors pretend in their press releases, but what developers are actually building—Datasette founder Simon Willison gets you pretty close to ground truth.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data breach exposes 400,000 bank customers’ info
item.description: Major Marquis fintech breach exposes 400,000-plus Americans' data through unpatched SonicWall vulnerability, with Texas hardest hit at 354,000 affected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
